title: cerastium viscosum var. glutinosum
intro: Cerastium viscosum var. glutinosum, also known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky chickweed, is a plant species within the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its sticky or viscid leaves and stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cerastium viscosum var. glutinosum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
